commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=95129293eb4641cf71b7be36614bf4c68c5ab194)
commands.review_summary:
The PR makes launchd bootstrap treat an `Operation already in progress` restart race as success after `launchctl print` confirms the Gateway LaunchAgent is loaded, and adds a regression test.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this review. Source inspection shows current main throws on this non-zero bootstrap path, and the linked report provides macOS log evidence, but I did not run a real concurrent launchd restart.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because the PR has a blocking correctness finding and insufficient contributor real-behavior proof that automation cannot supply for the contributor's macOS setup.

target.title: Handle concurrent launchd bootstrap restart race
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #84721.
- Treats a launchd bootstrap `Operation already in progress` race as successful when `launchctl print` confirms the Gateway LaunchAgent is loaded.
- Keeps the existing LaunchAgent `KeepAlive` policy intact; this only changes restart/bootstrap error handling.
- Adds a regression test for a concurrent bootstrap that loads the service while the current restart command receives the losing launchctl error.

## Real behavior proof

Local targeted test run:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/launchd.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  71 passed (71)
```

Observed local failure evidence before the source fix:

```text
Gateway restart failed: launchctl bootstrap failed: Bootstrap failed: 5: Input/output error
```

macOS unified log evidence from the same restart window showed a concurrent launchd bootstrap race rather than a Gateway crash:

```text
Bootstrap ... ai.openclaw.gateway ... succeeded
Bootstrap ... ai.openclaw.gateway ... failed: 37: Operation already in progress
Successfully spawned ai.openclaw.gateway
```

## Notes

The failed bootstrap can be the losing side of a concurrent restart while another OpenClaw process has already loaded the same LaunchAgent. Probing the service target after this specific launchctl error avoids surfacing a false restart failure while preserving the existing behavior for genuine bootstrap errors.
target.author: googlerest
